name: Ancient Astronaut
subtitle: Caveman name: Shiny Sleep Thing
body: They aren't hostile. They want to HELP. They keep trying to teach the cavemen to build a primitive battery. Every cooperative grunt fires up an excited teaching hologram. Every grab for a rock earns a sleep-ray "for the specimen's own safety."
Sleep ray: drop for 1d6 rounds, wake groggy. Nobody dies easily — that's the point.
The saucer can be BUILD-sabotaged, THROW-rocked, or simply swarmed by seven angry tribesfolk.
One keeps trying to shake hands. It does not understand why this provokes clubbing.

name: Cave Bear
subtitle: Caveman name: Big Hairy Cave Smelly
body: Also harvesting honey at the bee tree, and considers the tree his property. He would prefer you left. Loudly.
Run away — or gang-fell him with the whole clan for a real food haul. 32 Ungggh is beatable by seven cavemen, and they should earn it.
He isn't angry yet, just eating noisily. The tension is whether they notice before he does.

name: Giant Leech
subtitle: Caveman name: Smelly Water Thing
body: Lurks in the marsh shallows, always hungry. Latches on silently and drains 1 Ungggh per round until killed or pried off — a player narrates feeling "tired" before realizing why.
Tastes rancid. Anyone who tries to eat it rolls Resistance or is queasy. Bonk shrugs it off and is weirdly proud.

name: Lizard Person
subtitle: Caveman name: Smelly Cave Thing
body: Skulks in the marsh, watching the sky with open dread. It hisses and flees rather than fights — the Shiny Sleep Things abduct lizard people as well, and it knows exactly how this goes.
It's a witness, not a boss. Chasing it into the reeds risks a tar pit. Tastes rancid.
The reveal, delivered vaguely: "Shiny thing in sky. Go up. Go down. Cave-friend — not smelly now. Shiny. Sleep. Then nothing."

name: Random Opponent
subtitle: If solved too fast — roll 1d6
body: 2. Herbivore having a very bad day
